quizzes.title: HORT 235: Crossword Clues — Design & Techniques
quizzes.description: Answer the crossword clues from the HORT 235 final review. Type the answer for each clue.

data.term: Mnemonic for Principles of Design: Focus, Balance, Contrast, Rhythm, Unity, Scale, Harmony, Depth
data.answer: FBCRUSHD

data.term: Technique for wiring broad leaves like salal
data.answer: STITCH
TINT

data.term: Elements of Design mnemonic: Can Sally Sell Seashells From The Lake (Color, Shape, Size, Space, Fragrance, Texture, Line)
data.answer: DESIGN
hints: The building blocks of all artistic compositions
Color, Shape, Size, Space, Fragrance, Texture, Line
Can Sally Sell Seashells From The Lake

data.term: 3 colors next to each other on the color wheel
data.answer: ANALOGOUS
GYPSOPHILA

data.term: Making 2-3 mini carnations from one standard carnation
data.answer: FEATHERING
hints: Splitting a flower into smaller pieces

data.term: Wiring technique that wraps around the stem (for fillers)
data.answer: WRAPAROUND
ANTIRRHINUM
SHADE

data.term: Something that sucks all the moisture out (for preserving flowers)
data.answer: DESICCANT

data.term: Vertical line direction creates a feeling of ___ and masculinity
data.answer: STRENGTH

data.term: The empty space (like the hole in a donut)
data.answer: NEGATIVE

data.term: Physical balance is also called ___ balance
data.answer: MECHANICAL

data.term: How your eye travels through the design (another word for rhythm)
data.answer: MOVEMENT
hints: Your eye moves through the arrangement

data.answer: HELIANTHUS

quizzes.title: Cooking: Word Search Terms — Definitions
quizzes.description: Define these cooking terms from the word search. Type the definition or description for each term.

data.answer: Searing then slow-cooking in liquid in a covered pot
Cooking with dry heat in an oven, uncovered
Browning meat quickly at very high heat
hints: Often the first step in braising

data.answer: Cooking quickly in a small amount of fat over high heat
hints: Food moves quickly in the pan

data.answer: Briefly boiling then plunging into ice water
hints: Used to peel tomatoes or set color

data.answer: A mixture of two liquids that normally don't mix (like oil and vinegar)
Protein network formed when flour is mixed with water
Equal parts fat and flour cooked together as a thickener
Flavorful liquid made by simmering bones, vegetables, and aromatics
White sauce made from roux plus milk
hints: Used in mac and cheese, lasagna

data.answer: Sauce made from roux plus light stock (chicken, fish, or veal)
Light, airy cake or pie filling made with whipped egg whites
hints: Uses whipped egg whites for lift
A type of cake or pie

data.answer: Creamy mixture thickened with eggs (like creme brulee or flan)
Chemical browning reaction between amino acids and sugars when heated
hints: Why toast is brown and tasty

data.answer: Coating food lightly in flour or breadcrumbs before cooking
Letting yeast dough rise before baking
Cutting food into very tiny, fine pieces
White streaks of fat running through meat
Decorative crimped edge on a pie crust
Pasta cooked until firm to the bite, not mushy
The nutrient-rich embryo of a grain kernel
The tough outer layer of a grain kernel
Breaking down a whole animal carcass into usable cuts
Emulsion sauce made from egg yolks, butter, and lemon juice
Uncooked sauce of basil, garlic, pine nuts, Parmesan, and olive oil
Dough made from flour, fat, and water used for pies and tarts
Flavorful liquid made by simmering meat (not bones) with vegetables
hints: Like stock but from meat, not bones
